title: castalia flava
intro: Castalia flava, also known as the golden water lily or golden calla, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its striking golden flowers and shiny green leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Castalia flava include its potential to treat various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a diuretic and to treat kidney problems. Herbal preparations of Castalia flava include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
